CHAPTER ______
1 AN ACT concerning
2 Creation of a State Debt – Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loan of 2026,
3 and the Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loans of 2005, 2014, 2015, 2016,
4 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025
5 FOR the purpose of authorizing the creation of a State Debt in the amount of One Billion,
6 Eight Hundred and Twenty–Four Million, Seven Hundred and Eighty–Two
7 Thousand Dollars ($1,824,782,000) One Billion, Seven Hundred and Fifty–Eight
8 Million, Two Hundred and Sixty–One Thousand, Nine Hundred and Six Dollars
9 ($1,758,261,906) One Billion, Eight Hundred and Thirty–Nine Million, Nine
10 Hundred and Forty Thousand, Four Hundred and Six Dollars ($1,839,940,406) One
11 Billion, Eight Hundred and Forty–Seven Million, and Ninety–Nine Thousand
12 Dollars ($1,847,099,000), the proceeds to be used for certain necessary building,
13 construction, demolition, planning, renovation, conversion, replacement, and capital
14 equipment purchases of the State, for acquiring certain real estate in connection
15 therewith, and for grants to certain subdivisions and other organizations for certain
EXPLANATION: CAPITALS INDICATE MATTER ADDED TO EXISTING LAW.
[Brackets] indicate matter deleted from existing law.
Underlining indicates amendments to bill.
Strike out indicates matter stricken from the bill by amendment or deleted from the law by
amendment.

2 SENATE BILL 283
1 development and improvement purposes; subject to certain requirements that
2 certain matching funds be provided and expended by certain dates; providing
3 generally for the issuance and sale of bonds evidencing the loan; imposing a certain
4 tax on all assessable property in the State; specifying the use of certain school
5 construction funds; removing certain matching fund requirements; requiring that
6 certain grantees convey certain easements under certain circumstances to the
7 Maryland Historical Trust; providing that the proceeds of certain loans must be
8 expended or encumbered by a certain date; repealing requirements for certain
9 grantees to present evidence to the Board of Public Works that certain matching funds
10 will be provided on or before a certain date; providing that certain funds may be used
11 only for certain purposes; requiring the Department of General Services to record
12 certain correct information in certain grant agreements under certain
13 circumstances, notwithstanding certain differences; authorizing the Board of Public
14 Works, under certain circumstances, to approve certain appropriations,
15 notwithstanding certain differences; altering and clarifying certain provisions of law
16 relating to capital expenditures and grants for capital projects; repealing the
17 termination of authority to spend an appropriation for a capital project if certain
18 conditions are not met within a certain 2–year period, and making the repeal
19 applicable retroactively; exempting money authorized for certain programs from the
20 requirement that an authorization of State debt terminates 7 years after the date of
21 authorization, and making the exemption applicable retroactively; requiring certain
22 parking spaces allocated for employees of the General Assembly in a certain parking
23 facility to be allocated in a certain manner during a certain period of time each year;
24 authorizing certain unexpended appropriations in certain prior capital budgets and
25 bond loans to be expended for other public projects; altering certain requirements for
26 certain programs in certain prior capital budgets and bond loans; reducing prior
27 authorizations of State Debt; requiring that certain projects be constructed at certain
28 locations; adding, altering, and repealing certain requirements for certain
29 appropriations; altering and expanding the authorized purpose of certain grants;
30 providing that certain grants may not terminate before certain dates; authorizing
31 premiums from the sale of State bonds in certain fiscal years to be used for certain
32 purposes; requiring the Comptroller to make certain transfers, adjustments, and
33 reconciliations; repealing certain Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loan
34 Preauthorization acts; specifying the use of certain project funds; authorizing the
35 creation of State Debt in certain years to be used for certain purposes; clarifying the
36 use of certain bond proceeds; and generally relating to the financing of certain capital
37 projects.

10 Section 13, 14, 15, and 16
11 SECTION 1. BE IT ENACTED BY THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF MARYLAND,
12 That:
13 (1) The Board of Public Works may borrow money and incur indebtedness on
14 behalf of the State of Maryland through a State loan to be known as the Maryland
15 Consolidated Capital Bond Loan of 2026 in the total principal amount of $1,824,782,000
16 $1,758,261,906 $1,839,940,406 $1,847,099,000. This loan shall be evidenced by the
17 issuance, sale, and delivery of State general obligation bonds authorized by a resolution of
18 the Board of Public Works and issued, sold, and delivered in accordance with §§ 8–117
19 through 8–124 and 8–131.2 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
20 (2) The bonds to evidence this loan or installments of this loan may be sold as a
21 single issue of bonds under § 8–122 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
22 (3) The cash proceeds of the sale of the bonds shall be paid to the Treasurer and
23 first shall be applied to the payment of the expenses of issuing, selling, and delivering the
24 bonds, unless funds for this purpose are otherwise provided, and then shall be credited on
25 the books of the Comptroller and expended on approval by the Board of Public Works, for
26 the following public purposes, including any applicable architects’ and engineers’ fees:
27 EXECUTIVE DEPARTMENT – GOVERNOR

33 (a) Unless a school serves students through the
34 Non–Public Placement Program, an eligible school

1 may apply and qualify for a grant as specified below
2 based on the following criteria:
3 (1) At least 20% of the school’s
4 students are eligible for free
5 or reduced price meal
6 programs;

9 average per pupil
10 expenditure for public
11 schools as calculated by the

13 of Education; and

16 more; and

18 (1) All three of the criteria

22 specified above, the school
23 may receive up to $75,000;
24 and
25 (3) One of the three criteria
26 specified above, the school
27 may receive up to $25,000.

19 700,000
20 (4) An annual tax is imposed on all assessable property in the State in rate and
21 amount sufficient to pay the principal of and interest on the bonds, as and when due and
22 until paid in full. The principal shall be discharged within 15 years after the date of issue
23 of the bonds.
24 (5) (a) Prior to the payment of any matching grant funds under the provisions
25 of Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, grantees shall provide and expend
26 matching funds as specified. No part of a grantee’s matching fund may be provided, either
27 directly or indirectly, from funds of the State, whether appropriated or unappropriated.
28 Except as otherwise provided, no part of the fund may consist of real property, in kind
29 contributions, or funds expended prior to the effective date of this Act. In case of any dispute
30 as to what money or assets may qualify as matching funds, the Board of Public Works shall
31 determine the matter, and the Board’s decision is final. Grantees have until June 1, 2028,
32 to present evidence satisfactory to the Board of Public Works that the matching fund will
33 be provided. If satisfactory evidence is presented, the Board shall certify this fact to the
34 State Treasurer and the proceeds of the loan shall be expended for the purposes provided in
35 this Act. If this evidence is not presented by June 1, 2028, the proceeds of the loan shall be
36 applied to the purposes authorized in § 8–129 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
37 (b) It is further provided that when an equal and matching fund is specified
38 in Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, grantees shall provide a matching fund
39 equal to the lesser of (i) the authorized amount of the State grant or (ii) the amount of the
40 matching fund certified by the Board of Public Works. If satisfactory evidence is presented,
148 SENATE BILL 283
1 the Board shall certify this fact and the amount of the matching fund to the State Treasurer
2 and the proceeds of the loan equal to the amount of the matching fund shall be expended for
3 the purposes provided in this Act. If this evidence is not presented by June 1, 2028, the
4 proceeds of the loan shall be applied to the purposes authorized in § 8–129 of the State
5 Finance and Procurement Article. The proceeds of any amount of the loan in excess of the
6 matching fund certified by the Board of Public Works shall also be applied to the purposes
7 authorized in § 8–129 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
8 (6) (a) Prior to approval by the Board of Public Works of an expenditure of bond
9 proceeds authorized under Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, the grantee
10 shall grant and convey to the Maryland Historical Trust a historic preservation easement
11 on the property where the capital project assisted by the bond proceeds is located if the
12 Director of the Trust determines that the capital project impacts real property that is
13 individually listed in, or eligible for individual listing in, the Maryland Register of Historic
14 Properties, unless the Director of the Trust also determines that the real property:
15 (i) Is a type that is already adequately represented among the
16 Trust’s existing easement properties;
17 (ii) Is already subject to adequate protections of historic preservation
18 law or instrument; or
19 (iii) Has conditions peculiar to it that make requiring an easement
20 impractical.
21 (b) If the grantee holds a lease on the property, the Trust may accept an
22 easement on the leasehold interest.
23 (c) The easement must be in form, substance, and duration acceptable to
24 the Director of the Trust.
25 (d) (i) A recipient may administratively appeal to the Maryland
26 Historical Trust Board of Trustees a determination made by the Director of the Trust under
27 subparagraph (a) of this paragraph.
28 (ii) The decision made by the Maryland Historical Trust Board of
29 Trustees on an appeal is final and is not subject to further administrative appeal or judicial
30 review.
31 (7) The Except as provided in § 8–128(c) of the State Finance and Procurement
32 Article, the proceeds of the loan must be expended or encumbered by the Board of Public
33 Works for the purposes provided in this Act no later than June 1, 2033. If any funds
34 authorized by this Act remain unexpended or unencumbered after June 1, 2033, the amount
35 of the unexpended or unencumbered authorization shall be canceled and be of no further
36 force and effect.
37 (8) Multiple grants provided to the same organization in this Section are in
SENATE BILL 283 149
1 addition to one another. Unless otherwise provided, any matching fund requirements apply
2 to each individual grant.
3 (9) (a) Subject to subparagraph (b) of this paragraph, the Board of Public
4 Works may approve an appropriation in Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act
5 or in any other previously enacted Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loan Act,
6 notwithstanding differences in:
7 (i) The name of the grantee or the description of the project, provided
8 that the proposed use of funds is consistent with the public purpose of the original
9 appropriation; or
10 (ii) The location of the project.
11 (b) When the difference in the name of the grantee is nonsubstantive and
12 the identity of the grantee can be determined, and when the difference in the description of
13 the project is nonsubstantive and is consistent with the public purpose of the original
14 appropriation, the Department of General Services shall record the correct grantee
15 identification and project description in the grant agreement entered into by the grantee
16 with the State of Maryland and recorded with the Board of Public Works. In all other
17 instances of differences provided in subparagraph (a) of this paragraph, the Department of
18 Budget and Management shall notify the Office of Policy Analysis within the Department
19 of Legislative Services in writing of:
20 (i) The differences between an appropriation in Section 1(3) Items
21 ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act and the proposed use of the funds; and
22 (ii) The justification that the proposed use of the funds is consistent
23 with the public purpose of the appropriation.
24 (c) (i) The Office of Policy Analysis shall have 45 days to review and
25 comment on the proposed use of the funds.
26 (ii) If the Office of Policy Analysis does not submit written objections
27 within 45 days, the Department of Budget and Management shall provide certification in
28 writing to the Board of Public Works that the proposed use of funds may be approved
29 notwithstanding technical differences in the appropriation in Section 1(3) Items ZA00
30 through ZB02 of this Act.
31 (d) It is the intent of the General Assembly that the provisions of this
32 paragraph:
33 (i) Provide an administrative process authorizing the approval of
34 grants by the Board of Public Works, provided that the proposed use of funds is consistent
35 with the public purpose of the original appropriation; and
36 (ii) Be applicable to this Act and all prior Maryland Consolidated

8 in fiscal year years 2026 and 2027 to be transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund. Upon each
9 issuance of State bonds in fiscal year years 2026 and 2027, the Treasurer shall notify the
10 Comptroller of the Treasurer’s determination and the Comptroller shall credit the premium
11 accordingly. Premium from the sale of bonds in fiscal year years 2026 and 2027 that is not
12 transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund may, upon approval of the Board of Public Works, be
13 expended by the Comptroller for any of the public purposes set forth in Section 1(3) of this

23 Section 1(11)
24 Notwithstanding §§ 8–125(e) and 8–132 of the State Finance and Procurement
25 Article, the State Treasurer shall determine the amount of premium from the sale of bonds
26 in fiscal years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026 to be transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund.
27 Upon each issuance of State bonds in fiscal years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026, the
28 Treasurer shall notify the Comptroller of the Treasurer’s determination and the
29 Comptroller shall credit the premium accordingly. Premium from the sale of bonds in fiscal
30 years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026 that is not transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund may,
31 upon approval of the Board of Public Works, be expended by the Comptroller for any of the
32 public purposes set forth in Section 1(3) of this Act, including any applicable architects’ and
33 engineers’ fees.
34 SECTION 3. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the General Assembly
35 declares that it is the public policy of this State to manage State general obligation bond
36 debt in a manner that will maintain Maryland’s high quality credit rating. The General
37 Assembly further declares that legislative oversight, control, and review of all forms of
38 State obligations are essential to maintenance of the State’s existing bond rating and
39 protection of the fiscal integrity of the State.
SENATE BILL 283 183
1 SECTION 4. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, before work may commence
2 pursuant to any supplement to any appropriation contained in this Act, satisfactory
3 evidence must be given to the Board of Public Works that the project can be completed with
4 the aggregate of the funds in this Act and previously appropriated for the stated purpose.
5 SECTION 5. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That:
6 (1) with the approval of the Department of Budget and Management, any
7 appropriation for design provided in this Act may be used to fund construction if the amount
8 of the appropriation exceeds the amount required for design expenses, including allowances
9 for contingencies; and
10 (2) with the approval of the Department of Budget and Management, any
11 appropriation for construction provided in this Act may be used to purchase capital
12 equipment if the amount of the appropriation exceeds the amount required for construction
13 expenses, including allowances for contingencies.
14 SECTION 6. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, except as otherwise provided
15 in this Act, before a State agency or institution named in this Act as responsible for an
16 individual item may begin work with funds appropriated by this Act, the agency or
17 institution shall provide satisfactory evidence to the Board of Public Works that the work
18 described in the individual item can be completed with the funds specified for that item.
19 SECTION 7. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, with the approval of the
20 Department of Budget and Management, any appropriation under the provisions of this
21 Act that is in excess of the amount needed for a project may be credited to the Construction
22 Contingency Fund under § 3–609 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
23 Notwithstanding the fund balance restriction set forth in § 3–609(c) of the State Finance
24 and Procurement Article, any funds credited to the Fund may cause the total balance in
25 the Fund to exceed 1.25% of the capital debt affordability limit as determined under Title
26 8 of the State Finance and Procurement Article, in fiscal year 2027.
27 SECTION 8. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, if federal funds are available
28 to help accomplish any project identified in this Act, the State agency or institution
29 responsible for the project shall make efforts through proper administrative procedures to
30 obtain these federal funds. Before spending any funds appropriated by this Act, the agency
31 or institution shall certify its efforts to the Board of Public Works and state the reason for
32 any failure to obtain federal funds. If federal funds are obtained, they shall be used to defray
33 the costs of the project described in this Act and not to expand its scope.
34 SECTION 9. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That:
35 (1) for any appropriation for the planning of a State–owned project provided in
36 this Act, if a program required by § 3–602(d) of the State Finance and Procurement Article
37 has not been submitted, the State agency or institution responsible for the project shall
38 submit a program to the Department of Budget and Management for approval before funds
39 may be expended from the appropriation; and
184 SENATE BILL 283
1 (2) for any appropriation for the construction of a State–owned project provided
2 in this Act, if preliminary plans and outline specifications required by § 3–602(f)(2)(i) of the
3 State Finance and Procurement Article have not been prepared, the State agency or
4 institution responsible for the project shall submit preliminary plans and outline
5 specifications to the Department of Budget and Management for approval before funds may
6 be expended from the appropriation.
7 SECTION 10. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That no portion of the proceeds of
8 a loan or any of the matching funds provided for a project funded under this Act may be
9 used for the furtherance of an essentially religious endeavor. Upon the request of the Board
10 of Public Works, a recipient of the proceeds of a loan under this Act shall submit evidence
11 satisfactory to the Board that none of the proceeds of the loan or any matching funds have
12 been or are being used for a purpose prohibited by this Act.
13 SECTION 11. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the Comptroller may
14 advance funds to any loan funds account established pursuant to a general obligation bond
15 loan enabling Act for any expenditure authorized by that Act, provided that if general
16 obligation bonds have not been issued under the authority of that Act, the next ensuing
17 sale of general obligation bonds shall include the issuance of bonds under the authority of
18 that Act in an amount at least equivalent to the amount of the funds so advanced.
19 SECTION 12. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That Section(s) 13, 14, 15, and 16
20 of Chapter 603 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 2025 be repealed.

SENATE BILL 283 205
1 SECTION 17. 21. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the Board of Public
2 Works may approve contracts and otherwise obligate funds authorized in this Act upon the
3 effective date of the relevant section of this Act.
4 SECTION 18. 22. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That Section 13 of this Act
5 shall take effect June 1, 2027.
6 SECTION 19. 23. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That Section 14 of this Act
7 shall take effect June 1, 2028.
8 SECTION 20. 24. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That Section 15 of this Act
9 shall take effect June 1, 2029.
10 SECTION 21. 25. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, except as provided in
11 Sections 18, 19, and 20 22, 23, and 24 of this Act, this Act shall take effect June 1, 2026.

1 AN ACT concerning
2 Creation of a State Debt – Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loan of 2026,
3 and the Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loans of 2005, 2014, 2015, 2016,
4 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025
5 FOR the purpose of authorizing the creation of a State Debt in the amount of One Billion,
6 Eight Hundred and Twenty–Four Million, Seven Hundred and Eighty–Two
7 Thousand Dollars ($1,824,782,000) One Billion, Seven Hundred and Fifty–Eight
8 Million, Two Hundred and Sixty–One Thousand, Nine Hundred and Six Dollars
9 ($1,758,261,906) One Billion, Eight Hundred and Thirty–Nine Million, Nine
10 Hundred and Forty Thousand, Four Hundred and Six Dollars ($1,839,940,406) One
11 Billion, Eight Hundred and Forty–Seven Million, and Ninety–Nine Thousand
12 Dollars ($1,847,099,000), the proceeds to be used for certain necessary building,
13 construction, demolition, planning, renovation, conversion, replacement, and capital
14 equipment purchases of the State, for acquiring certain real estate in connection
15 therewith, and for grants to certain subdivisions and other organizations for certain

1 development and improvement purposes; subject to certain requirements that
2 certain matching funds be provided and expended by certain dates; providing
3 generally for the issuance and sale of bonds evidencing the loan; imposing a certain
4 tax on all assessable property in the State; specifying the use of certain school
5 construction funds; removing certain matching fund requirements; requiring that

16 relating to capital expenditures and grants for capital projects; repealing the
17 termination of authority to spend an appropriation for a capital project if certain
18 conditions are not met within a certain 2–year period, and making the repeal
19 applicable retroactively; exempting money authorized for certain programs from the
20 requirement that an authorization of State debt terminates 7 years after the date of

22 parking spaces allocated for employees of the General Assembly in a certain parking
23 facility to be allocated in a certain manner during a certain period of time each year;
24 authorizing certain unexpended appropriations in certain prior capital budgets and
25 bond loans to be expended for other public projects; altering certain requirements for
26 certain programs in certain prior capital budgets and bond loans; reducing prior
27 authorizations of State Debt; requiring that certain projects be constructed at certain

29 appropriations; altering and expanding the authorized purpose of certain grants;
30 providing that certain grants may not terminate before certain dates; authorizing
31 premiums from the sale of State bonds in certain fiscal years to be used for certain
32 purposes; requiring the Comptroller to make certain transfers, adjustments, and
33 reconciliations; repealing certain Maryland Consolidated Capital Bond Loan
34 Preauthorization acts; specifying the use of certain project funds; authorizing the
35 creation of State Debt in certain years to be used for certain purposes; clarifying the
36 use of certain bond proceeds; and generally relating to the financing of certain capital
37 projects.

10 Section 13, 14, 15, and 16
11 SECTION 1. BE IT ENACTED BY THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF MARYLAND,
12 That:
13 (1) The Board of Public Works may borrow money and incur indebtedness on
14 behalf of the State of Maryland through a State loan to be known as the Maryland
15 Consolidated Capital Bond Loan of 2026 in the total principal amount of $1,824,782,000
16 $1,758,261,906 $1,839,940,406 $1,847,099,000. This loan shall be evidenced by the
17 issuance, sale, and delivery of State general obligation bonds authorized by a resolution of
18 the Board of Public Works and issued, sold, and delivered in accordance with §§ 8–117
19 through 8–124 and 8–131.2 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
20 (2) The bonds to evidence this loan or installments of this loan may be sold as a
21 single issue of bonds under § 8–122 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
22 (3) The cash proceeds of the sale of the bonds shall be paid to the Treasurer and
23 first shall be applied to the payment of the expenses of issuing, selling, and delivering the
24 bonds, unless funds for this purpose are otherwise provided, and then shall be credited on
25 the books of the Comptroller and expended on approval by the Board of Public Works, for
26 the following public purposes, including any applicable architects’ and engineers’ fees:
27 EXECUTIVE DEPARTMENT – GOVERNOR
28 D25E03.02 INTERAGENCY COMMISSION ON SCHOOL
29 CONSTRUCTION
30 (A) Nancy K. Kopp Public School Facilities Priority Fund. Provide
31 grants to schools in accordance with § 5–326 of the Education
32 Article (Statewide) ....................................................................... 70,000,000
33 Provided that notwithstanding any other provision of law, this
34 authorization shall be allocated as follows:
35 (1) Senate–directed allocation ........................... 35,000,000

1 (2) House of Delegates–directed allocation ....... 35,000,000
2 (1) Anne Arundel County ................................... 3,000,000
3 (2) Baltimore City ............................................... 16,000,000
4 (3) Baltimore County .......................................... 5,000,000
5 (4) Frederick County ........................................... 5,000,000
6 (5) Howard County ............................................. 6,000,000
7 (6) Montgomery County ...................................... 15,000,000
8 (7) Prince George’s County ................................. 19,000,000
9 (8) All other jurisdictions ................................... 1,000,000
10 Further provided that these authorizations shall not be subject
11 to a local matching requirement.
12 (B) Public School Construction Program. Provide funds to
13 construct public school buildings and public school capital
14 improvements, including providing grants to local boards of
15 education for federal E–rate–eligible special construction such
16 as fiber and broadband infrastructure projects for
17 E–rate–eligible applicants in accordance with Title 5, Subtitle
18 3 of the Education Article (Statewide) ....................................... 300,000,000
19 (C) Senator James E. “Ed” DeGrange Nonpublic Aging Schools
20 Program. Provide funds to be distributed as grants to nonpublic
21 schools in Maryland for expenditures eligible under the Aging
22 Schools Program, including school security improvements.
23 Provided that grants may be provided only to nonpublic schools
24 eligible to receive Aid to Non–Public Schools R00A03.04 (for the
25 purchase of textbooks or computer hardware and software for
26 loans to students in eligible nonpublic schools) during the
27 2025–2026 school year or nonpublic schools that serve students
28 with disabilities through the Non–Public Placement Program
29 R00A02.07 Subprogram 0762, excluding preschools in fiscal
30 2027, with a maximum amount of $100,000 and a minimum
31 amount of $5,000 per eligible school.
32 Further provided that:
33 (a) Unless a school serves students through the
34 Non–Public Placement Program, an eligible school

SENATE BILL 283 9
1 may apply and qualify for a grant as specified below
2 based on the following criteria:
3 (1) At least 20% of the school’s
4 students are eligible for free
5 or reduced price meal
6 programs;
7 (2) Tuition charged to students
8 is less than the statewide
9 average per pupil
10 expenditure for public
11 schools as calculated by the
12 Maryland State Department
13 of Education; and
14 (3) The school has a facility with
15 an average age of 50 years or
16 more; and
17 (b) If a school meets:
18 (1) All three of the criteria
19 specified above, the school
20 may receive up to $100,000;
21 (2) Two of the three criteria
22 specified above, the school
23 may receive up to $75,000;
24 and
25 (3) One of the three criteria
26 specified above, the school
27 may receive up to $25,000.
28 Further provided that if more eligible schools apply and qualify
29 for grants than the total authorizations, the Maryland State
30 Department of Education shall prioritize awarding full grant
31 amounts to residential school applicants participating in the
32 Non–Public Placement Program and shall prorate the grants
33 for the remaining applicants based on the total remaining total
34 authorization amount. Further provided that the funds shall be
35 administered by the Maryland State Department of Education
36 and the Interagency Commission on School Construction.
37 Further provided that grants made to nonpublic schools shall
38 be expended within 3 years of the date that funding for the
39 grants became available. Any funding for grants that is

20 (4) An annual tax is imposed on all assessable property in the State in rate and
21 amount sufficient to pay the principal of and interest on the bonds, as and when due and
22 until paid in full. The principal shall be discharged within 15 years after the date of issue
23 of the bonds.
24 (5) (a) Prior to the payment of any matching grant funds under the provisions
25 of Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, grantees shall provide and expend
26 matching funds as specified. No part of a grantee’s matching fund may be provided, either
27 directly or indirectly, from funds of the State, whether appropriated or unappropriated.
28 Except as otherwise provided, no part of the fund may consist of real property, in kind
29 contributions, or funds expended prior to the effective date of this Act. In case of any dispute
30 as to what money or assets may qualify as matching funds, the Board of Public Works shall
31 determine the matter, and the Board’s decision is final. Grantees have until June 1, 2028,
32 to present evidence satisfactory to the Board of Public Works that the matching fund will
33 be provided. If satisfactory evidence is presented, the Board shall certify this fact to the
34 State Treasurer and the proceeds of the loan shall be expended for the purposes provided in
35 this Act. If this evidence is not presented by June 1, 2028, the proceeds of the loan shall be
36 applied to the purposes authorized in § 8–129 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
37 (b) It is further provided that when an equal and matching fund is specified
38 in Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, grantees shall provide a matching fund
39 equal to the lesser of (i) the authorized amount of the State grant or (ii) the amount of the
40 matching fund certified by the Board of Public Works. If satisfactory evidence is presented,

148 SENATE BILL 283
1 the Board shall certify this fact and the amount of the matching fund to the State Treasurer
2 and the proceeds of the loan equal to the amount of the matching fund shall be expended for
3 the purposes provided in this Act. If this evidence is not presented by June 1, 2028, the
4 proceeds of the loan shall be applied to the purposes authorized in § 8–129 of the State
5 Finance and Procurement Article. The proceeds of any amount of the loan in excess of the
6 matching fund certified by the Board of Public Works shall also be applied to the purposes
7 authorized in § 8–129 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
8 (6) (a) Prior to approval by the Board of Public Works of an expenditure of bond
9 proceeds authorized under Section 1(3) Items ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act, the grantee
10 shall grant and convey to the Maryland Historical Trust a historic preservation easement
11 on the property where the capital project assisted by the bond proceeds is located if the
12 Director of the Trust determines that the capital project impacts real property that is
13 individually listed in, or eligible for individual listing in, the Maryland Register of Historic
14 Properties, unless the Director of the Trust also determines that the real property:
15 (i) Is a type that is already adequately represented among the
16 Trust’s existing easement properties;
17 (ii) Is already subject to adequate protections of historic preservation
18 law or instrument; or
19 (iii) Has conditions peculiar to it that make requiring an easement
20 impractical.
21 (b) If the grantee holds a lease on the property, the Trust may accept an
22 easement on the leasehold interest.
23 (c) The easement must be in form, substance, and duration acceptable to
24 the Director of the Trust.
25 (d) (i) A recipient may administratively appeal to the Maryland
26 Historical Trust Board of Trustees a determination made by the Director of the Trust under
27 subparagraph (a) of this paragraph.
28 (ii) The decision made by the Maryland Historical Trust Board of
29 Trustees on an appeal is final and is not subject to further administrative appeal or judicial
30 review.

9 appropriation; or
10 (ii) The location of the project.
11 (b) When the difference in the name of the grantee is nonsubstantive and
12 the identity of the grantee can be determined, and when the difference in the description of
13 the project is nonsubstantive and is consistent with the public purpose of the original
14 appropriation, the Department of General Services shall record the correct grantee
15 identification and project description in the grant agreement entered into by the grantee
16 with the State of Maryland and recorded with the Board of Public Works. In all other
17 instances of differences provided in subparagraph (a) of this paragraph, the Department of
18 Budget and Management shall notify the Office of Policy Analysis within the Department
19 of Legislative Services in writing of:
20 (i) The differences between an appropriation in Section 1(3) Items
21 ZA00 through ZB02 of this Act and the proposed use of the funds; and
22 (ii) The justification that the proposed use of the funds is consistent
23 with the public purpose of the appropriation.
24 (c) (i) The Office of Policy Analysis shall have 45 days to review and
25 comment on the proposed use of the funds.
26 (ii) If the Office of Policy Analysis does not submit written objections
27 within 45 days, the Department of Budget and Management shall provide certification in
28 writing to the Board of Public Works that the proposed use of funds may be approved
29 notwithstanding technical differences in the appropriation in Section 1(3) Items ZA00
30 through ZB02 of this Act.

23 Section 1(11)
24 Notwithstanding §§ 8–125(e) and 8–132 of the State Finance and Procurement
25 Article, the State Treasurer shall determine the amount of premium from the sale of bonds
26 in fiscal years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026 to be transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund.
27 Upon each issuance of State bonds in fiscal years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026, the
28 Treasurer shall notify the Comptroller of the Treasurer’s determination and the
29 Comptroller shall credit the premium accordingly. Premium from the sale of bonds in fiscal
30 years 2024 [and], 2025, AND 2026 that is not transferred to the Annuity Bond Fund may,
31 upon approval of the Board of Public Works, be expended by the Comptroller for any of the
32 public purposes set forth in Section 1(3) of this Act, including any applicable architects’ and
33 engineers’ fees.
34 SECTION 3. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the General Assembly
35 declares that it is the public policy of this State to manage State general obligation bond
36 debt in a manner that will maintain Maryland’s high quality credit rating. The General
37 Assembly further declares that legislative oversight, control, and review of all forms of
38 State obligations are essential to maintenance of the State’s existing bond rating and
39 protection of the fiscal integrity of the State.

SENATE BILL 283 183
1 SECTION 4. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, before work may commence
2 pursuant to any supplement to any appropriation contained in this Act, satisfactory
3 evidence must be given to the Board of Public Works that the project can be completed with
4 the aggregate of the funds in this Act and previously appropriated for the stated purpose.
5 SECTION 5. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That:
6 (1) with the approval of the Department of Budget and Management, any
7 appropriation for design provided in this Act may be used to fund construction if the amount
8 of the appropriation exceeds the amount required for design expenses, including allowances
9 for contingencies; and
10 (2) with the approval of the Department of Budget and Management, any
11 appropriation for construction provided in this Act may be used to purchase capital
12 equipment if the amount of the appropriation exceeds the amount required for construction
13 expenses, including allowances for contingencies.
14 SECTION 6. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, except as otherwise provided
15 in this Act, before a State agency or institution named in this Act as responsible for an
16 individual item may begin work with funds appropriated by this Act, the agency or
17 institution shall provide satisfactory evidence to the Board of Public Works that the work
18 described in the individual item can be completed with the funds specified for that item.
19 SECTION 7. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, with the approval of the
20 Department of Budget and Management, any appropriation under the provisions of this
21 Act that is in excess of the amount needed for a project may be credited to the Construction
22 Contingency Fund under § 3–609 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
23 Notwithstanding the fund balance restriction set forth in § 3–609(c) of the State Finance
24 and Procurement Article, any funds credited to the Fund may cause the total balance in
25 the Fund to exceed 1.25% of the capital debt affordability limit as determined under Title
26 8 of the State Finance and Procurement Article, in fiscal year 2027.
27 SECTION 8. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That, if federal funds are available
28 to help accomplish any project identified in this Act, the State agency or institution
29 responsible for the project shall make efforts through proper administrative procedures to
30 obtain these federal funds. Before spending any funds appropriated by this Act, the agency
31 or institution shall certify its efforts to the Board of Public Works and state the reason for
32 any failure to obtain federal funds. If federal funds are obtained, they shall be used to defray
33 the costs of the project described in this Act and not to expand its scope.
34 SECTION 9. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That:
35 (1) for any appropriation for the planning of a State–owned project provided in
36 this Act, if a program required by § 3–602(d) of the State Finance and Procurement Article
37 has not been submitted, the State agency or institution responsible for the project shall
38 submit a program to the Department of Budget and Management for approval before funds
39 may be expended from the appropriation; and

184 SENATE BILL 283
1 (2) for any appropriation for the construction of a State–owned project provided
2 in this Act, if preliminary plans and outline specifications required by § 3–602(f)(2)(i) of the
3 State Finance and Procurement Article have not been prepared, the State agency or
4 institution responsible for the project shall submit preliminary plans and outline
5 specifications to the Department of Budget and Management for approval before funds may
6 be expended from the appropriation.
7 SECTION 10. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That no portion of the proceeds of
8 a loan or any of the matching funds provided for a project funded under this Act may be
9 used for the furtherance of an essentially religious endeavor. Upon the request of the Board
10 of Public Works, a recipient of the proceeds of a loan under this Act shall submit evidence
11 satisfactory to the Board that none of the proceeds of the loan or any matching funds have
12 been or are being used for a purpose prohibited by this Act.
13 SECTION 11. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the Comptroller may
14 advance funds to any loan funds account established pursuant to a general obligation bond
15 loan enabling Act for any expenditure authorized by that Act, provided that if general
16 obligation bonds have not been issued under the authority of that Act, the next ensuing
17 sale of general obligation bonds shall include the issuance of bonds under the authority of
18 that Act in an amount at least equivalent to the amount of the funds so advanced.
19 SECTION 12. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That Section(s) 13, 14, 15, and 16
20 of Chapter 603 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 2025 be repealed.

27 authorized by a resolution of the Board of Public Works and issued, sold, and delivered in
28 accordance with §§ 8–117 through 8–124 and 8–131.2 of the State Finance and
29 Procurement Article.
30 (2) The bonds to evidence these loans or installments of these loans may be sold
31 as a single issue or may be consolidated and sold as part of a single issue of bonds under §
32 8–122 of the State Finance and Procurement Article.
33 (3) The cash proceeds of the sale of the bonds shall be paid to the Treasurer and
34 first shall be applied to the payment of the expenses of issuing, selling, and delivering the
35 bonds, unless funds for this purpose are otherwise provided, and then shall be credited on
36 the books of the Comptroller and expended, on approval by the Board of Public Works, for
37 the following public purposes, including any applicable architects’ and engineers’ fees:

13 SECTION 16. AND BE IT FURTHER ENACTED, That the Laws of Maryland read
14 as follows:
15 Article – State Finance and Procurement
16 7–305.
17 (a) (1) In this section[, “capital] THE FOLLOWING WORDS HAVE THE
18 MEANINGS INDICATED.
19 (2) “CAPITAL expenditure” includes an expenditure for:
20 [(1)] (I) acquisition of land, buildings, or equipment; or
21 [(2)] (II) new construction.
22 (3) “MISCELLANEOUS CAPITAL GRANT” MEANS A GRANT FOR A
23 CAPITAL PROJECT FOR AN ENTITY THAT IS NOT A UNIT OF STATE GOVERNMENT
24 FUNDED IN THE ANNUAL STATE OPERATING BUDGET WITHIN THE DEPARTMENT OF
25 GENERAL SERVICES.
26 (b) At the end of a fiscal year, the unspent part of an appropriation for a capital
27 expenditure made through the State budget or a supplementary appropriation act other than
28 an enabling act for a general obligation loan:
29 (1) does not revert to the General Fund; and

1 (2) with the approval of the Board of Public Works, may be carried in a
2 capital account until it is spent in accordance with subsection (c) of this section or until the
3 authority to spend the appropriation for a project terminates.
4 (c) Money carried in a capital account under subsection (b) of this section may be
5 spent only for a capital purpose.
6 (d) Unless otherwise provided by law, the authority to spend the appropriation for
7 a project terminates:
8 (1) [2 years after the effective date of the appropriation act that authorizes
9 the project, if:
10 (i) evidence that a required matching fund will be provided has not
11 been presented to the Board; or
12 (ii) no part of the project is under contract and the Board has not
13 encumbered money for any part of the project;
14 (2)] 1 year after the latest of abandonment, completion, or acceptance of the
15 project; or
16 [(3)] (2) except to the extent the Board has encumbered money for a State
17 project or program authorized by an appropriation act, 7 years after the effective date of the
18 appropriation act that authorized the project unless:
19 (i) the appropriation act provides otherwise; or
20 (ii) in an emergency, the Board unanimously grants a temporary
21 exception for 1 year.

17 (b) Unless otherwise provided in an enabling act, an authorization of State debt
18 for a project or program shall terminate no later than 1 year after the abandonment,
19 completion, or acceptance of the project or program, as determined by the Board.
20 (c) Except to the extent that money authorized by an enabling act for a State project
21 or program has been encumbered by the Board, an authorization of State debt shall
22 automatically terminate 7 years after the date of the authorization, unless:
23 (1) the enabling act provides otherwise; [or]
24 (2) in an emergency, the Board unanimously grants a temporary exception
25 for 1 year; OR
26 (3) THE AUTHORIZATION IS FOR A PROGRAM THAT:
27 (I) IS ADMINISTERED BY A UNIT OF STATE GOVERNMENT WHICH

202 SENATE BILL 283
1 (II) RECEIVES FUNDS THAT MAY BE REALLOCATED BY THE UNIT
2 BETWEEN PROJECTS WITHOUT REQUIRING A BUDGET AMENDMENT OR AN ACT OF

5 3 YEARS OR MORE AFTER THE EFFECTIVE DATE OF THE ENABLING ACT THAT
6 AUTHORIZED THE GRANT.

11 (I) NO PART OF THE GRANT IS UNDER CONTRACT APPROVED BY
12 THE BOARD; AND
13 (II) THE BOARD HAS NOT ENCUMBERED MONEY FOR ANY PART
14 OF THE GRANT.
15 [(d)] (E) If [bonds have not been issued] AN AUTHORIZATION IS TERMINATED
16 IN ACCORDANCE WITH THIS SECTION, the amount of any unissued bonds [from a]
17 SPECIFICALLY INTENDED FOR THE terminated authorization shall be canceled and be of
18 no further effect.

20 SECTION AND bonds have been issued SPECIFICALLY TO FUND THE AUTHORIZATION,
21 the amount of any unspent bond proceeds [from a] ASSOCIATED WITH THE terminated
22 authorization shall be disposed of as provided in § 8–129 of this subtitle.
23 [(f)] (G) (1) The Board shall enforce the provisions of this section.
24 (2) A failure to comply with or give effect to the provisions of this section
25 may not affect the validity or enforceability of State debt.
26 [(g)] (H) The Board shall submit for publication in the Maryland Register a
27 notice of any authorization of State debt that has been terminated or extended under this
28 section; the notice shall include the chapter number and year of enactment of the enabling
29 act and each amendment to the enabling act.
30 [(h)] (I) A termination or extension under this section may become effective only
31 after the publication of notice in the Maryland Register.
